About: It is about the Mother of God.
Affects: It affects those who suffer from alcoholism.
It affects those who suffer from drug abuse.
It affects those who suffer from other forms of addiction.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Russian Orthodox Church.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Vladychny Convent in Serpukhov.
ComposedBy: It was composed by Russian and English writers.
ConsistsOf: It consists of the Theotokos with hands raised in the orans position.
It consists of the Christ Child standing in a chalice with both hands raised in blessing.
CreatedBy: It was created by a peasant of the Tula province.
CreatedIn: It was created in Serpukhov, Russia.
CreatedTo: It was created to heal those who suffer from alcoholism.
It was created to heal those who suffer from drug abuse.
It was created to heal those who suffer from other forms of addiction.
DiscoveredAt: It was discovered at the Vladychny Convent in Serpukhov.
DiscoveredBy: It was discovered by a peasant of the Tula province.
HasA: It has a commemoration entered into the official liturgical calendar of the Russian Orthodox Church.
It has a feast day listed as May 5 O.S./May 18 N.S.
It has a feast day celebrated on November 27 O.S./December 10 N.S.
It has an akathist composed in Russian and English.
It has a molieben composed in Russian and English.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by healing those who suffer from alcoholism.
It is characterized by healing those who suffer from drug abuse.
It is characterized by healing those who suffer from other forms of addiction.
HeldIn: It was held in St. Nicholas Cathedral.
It is now venerated in the Vysotsky Monastery.
It is enshrined in the Vladychny Convent.
IsA: It is a wonderworking icon of the Mother of God.
It is a variant of the icon of Our Lady of Nicea.
It is a copy painted in the Byzantine style.
It is a copy painted in the "academic" style.
It is a skete dedicated to the Inexhaustible Chalice.
KnownAs: It is known as Inexhaustible Cup.
It is known as Non-intoxicating Chalice.
It is known as The Inexhaustible Cup.
It is known as The Inexhaustible Chalice.
KnownFor: It is known for healing those who suffer from alcoholism.
It is known for healing those who suffer from drug abuse.
It is known for healing those who suffer from other forms of addiction.
LocatedIn: It is located in Serpukhov, Russia.
It is located in the Vysotsky Monastery.
It is located in the Vladychny Convent.
MemberOf: It is a member of the Fellowship of the Inexhaustible Cup.
OccurredAt: It occurred at the Vladychny Convent in Serpukhov.
PartOf: It is part of the Russian Orthodox Church.
It is part of the Alexander Nevsky Cathedral in Serpakhov.
It is part of the Pokrov-Tervenichesky convent.
UsedAs: It is used as a shrine.
It is used as a recovery program.
UsedFor: It is used for healing from addiction.
It is used for intercessory prayer.
It is used for support among members to combat destructive forces of addictions.
UsedIn: It is used in recovery programs.
It is used in the Fellowship of the Inexhaustible Cup.
UsedTo: It is used to combat the many destructive forces of addictions.
WrittenBy: It was written by Jacob Ivanovich Brilliantov.
